Day 30, the last real day of the series. A quick wrap-up on something we've been quietly using the whole time: the new keyword and function constructors. We'll look at why new always gives you an object, how it changes what this points to, and how building an object with new compares to the plain object literal approach.

JS From Zero, Day 29: Template Strings Will Spoil You Forever

Day 29 of my reincarnated JS journey. This post covers ES6 Template Strings — those backtick strings with `${}` interpolation. I'll show you why string concatenation with `+` gets painful fast, and how Template Strings make building strings (especially long HTML chunks) so much nicer.
/en/javascript/2019/10/13/js-from-zero-28-es6-loops/
Learning JS From Zero, Day 28: ES6 Loops With forEach and for...of

Day 28 of my reincarnation into JavaScript. This time we look at two ES6 ways to loop over arrays: forEach and for...of. I'll show how much shorter they are than a classic for loop, how to trim forEach down with arrow functions, and when I reach for for...of versus for...in.
/en/javascript/2019/10/12/js-from-zero-ep27-es6-arrow-functions/
ES6 Arrow Functions: Less Typing, But Watch Out for `this`

Day 27 of being reincarnated into JS. This post covers ES6 arrow functions — how they let you write less code, and the one gotcha you really need to know: how `this` behaves differently inside an arrow function.
/en/javascript/2019/10/11/js-from-zero-26-es6-let-const/
ES6 let & const: Why You Should Stop Using var (And What the Temporal Dead Zone Is)

Day 26 of my reincarnated JS journey, and we're finally getting into ES6. First up: `let` and `const`. We'll look at how they fix variable pollution, why `const` won't let you reassign, and that weird thing called the temporal dead zone that trips people up when they think `let` and `const` get hoisted.
/en/javascript/2019/10/10/js-from-zero-25-callback-hell/
Learning JS From Zero (Day 25): What Exactly Is Callback Hell?

Day 25 of my reincarnation. This one's a quick look at what Callback Hell actually is — why it happens, why it's so painful to debug, and where the whole "pyramid of doom" shape comes from. It's not a JavaScript-only thing either, which surprises a lot of people. And yes, there's a way out.
/en/javascript/2019/10/09/js-from-zero-24-undefined-vs-not-defined/
JavaScript's undefined vs not defined: What's the Actual Difference?

Day 24 of my JS journey. undefined and not defined look similar and trip up a lot of beginners, but they mean very different things. This post breaks down what undefined actually is, how it ties back to Hoisting, and why not defined just means "this thing doesn't exist."
/en/javascript/2019/10/08/re-zero-js-day-23-hoisting/
RE: Zero JS Day 23 — What Is Hoisting, Really?

Day 23 of my from-scratch JavaScript journey. This time we dig into Hoisting — why you can call a function before it's even defined and still not get an error, the two phases JavaScript runs through, and why functions get hoisted ahead of variables.
/en/javascript/2019/10/07/js-from-zero-22-execution-context/
JS From Zero (Day 22): Execution Context, Variable Environment & the Scope Chain

Day 22 of my reincarnated JS journey. This time we cover four things that all tie together: functions, the execution context, the variable environment, and the scope chain. We'll look at why window sits at the top, how a variable's location decides where it lives, and how JavaScript searches outward to resolve a variable.
/en/javascript/2019/10/06/js-from-zero-21-date-time/
JS From Zero — Day 21: Working With Dates and Time

Day 21 of getting reincarnated into JavaScript. This time we're tackling dates and time — how to build a Date object, pull out the year, month, day, hours, minutes, seconds, milliseconds, and the timestamp. We'll also cover the two gotchas everyone trips on (getMonth and getDay start at 0) and how to assemble it all into a readable date string.
